questions.text: What is the degree of a linear polynomial?
questions.explanation: A linear polynomial is of the form ax + b, where the highest power of the variable x is 1.

questions.text: A quadratic polynomial has a degree of:
questions.explanation: A polynomial of degree 2 is called a quadratic polynomial.

questions.text: The maximum number of zeros a cubic polynomial can have is:
questions.explanation: A polynomial of degree n can have at most n zeros. A cubic polynomial has degree 3.

questions.text: The graph of a quadratic polynomial y = ax² + bx + c is a:
questions.explanation: The geometrical representation of a quadratic equation is a U-shaped curve called a parabola.

questions.text: If the graph of y = p(x) intersects the x-axis at exactly three points, then the number of zeros of p(x) is:
questions.explanation: The number of zeros is equal to the number of points where the graph intersects the x-axis.

questions.text: If α and β are the zeros of the quadratic polynomial ax² + bx + c, then α + β equals:
questions.explanation: The sum of zeros of a quadratic polynomial is given by the formula -coefficient of x / coefficient of x².

questions.text: If α and β are the zeros of the quadratic polynomial ax² + bx + c, then αβ equals:
questions.explanation: The product of zeros of a quadratic polynomial is given by the constant term / coefficient of x².

questions.text: Find the zeros of the polynomial x² - 3.
The zeros of the quadratic polynomial x² + 7x + 10 are:
A quadratic polynomial whose sum and product of zeros are -3 and 2 respectively is:
questions.explanation: Formula: x² - (sum)x + product. x² - (-3)x + 2 = x² + 3x + 2.

questions.text: If one zero of the polynomial x² - 4x + 1 is 2 + √3, then the other zero is:
questions.explanation: For quadratic polynomials with rational coefficients, irrational zeros occur in conjugate pairs.

questions.text: If the sum of the zeros of the polynomial kx² + 2x + 3k is equal to their product, then k is:
The degree of the zero polynomial is:
questions.explanation: The degree of the zero polynomial is not defined.

questions.text: If one zero of the quadratic polynomial x² + 3x + k is 2, then the value of k is:
A polynomial of degree 5 can have at most how many zeros?
questions.explanation: The number of zeros of a polynomial is at most equal to its degree.

questions.text: If α and β are zeros of p(x) = x² - x - 4, then the value of 1/α + 1/β is:
If the zeros of the quadratic polynomial x² + (a + 1)x + b are 2 and -3, then:
The number of polynomials having zeros as -2 and 5 is:
questions.explanation: Multiple polynomials can have the same zeros by multiplying the base polynomial k(x² - 3x - 10) by different constants k.

questions.text: If α, β, γ are the zeros of ax³ + bx² + cx + d, then α + β + γ is:
questions.explanation: Sum of zeros of a cubic polynomial is -b/a.

questions.text: The value of the polynomial p(x) = 5x - 4x² + 3 at x = -1 is:
Which of the following is not a polynomial?
questions.explanation: x + 1/x has a negative exponent (x⁻¹), so it is not a polynomial.

questions.text: If the graph of a polynomial does not intersect the x-axis but is above it, the number of real zeros is:
questions.explanation: Zeros are the x-coordinates of the points where the graph meets the x-axis.

questions.text: If one zero of the quadratic polynomial (k-1)x² + kx + 1 is -3, then the value of k is:
Zeros of p(x) = x² - 2x are:
questions.explanation: x(x-2) = 0 gives x=0 and x=2.

questions.text: If α and β are the zeros of p(x) = 4x² + 3x + 7, then 1/α + 1/β is:
The constant polynomial p(x) = 5 has how many zeros?
questions.explanation: A non-zero constant polynomial has no zeros because it never equals zero.

questions.text: If α and β are the zeros of f(x) = x² - p(x + 1) - c, then (α + 1)(β + 1) is:
If the sum of zeros of x² - kx + 6 is 3, then k is:
questions.explanation: Sum of zeros = -(-k)/1 = k. Since sum is 3, k = 3.

questions.text: If the zeros of the quadratic polynomial ax² + bx + c, c ≠ 0 are equal, then:
options: c and a have opposite signs
c and b have opposite signs
c and a have the same sign
c and b have the same sign
questions.explanation: For equal zeros, Discriminant D = b² - 4ac = 0. Since b² is positive, 4ac must be positive, meaning a and c must have the same sign.

questions.text: If one of the zeros of a cubic polynomial x³ + ax² + bx + c is -1, then the product of the other two zeros is:
questions.explanation: Substitute x=-1: -1 + a - b + c = 0 => c = 1 - a + b. Product of zeros is -c/(-1) = c? No, for cubic, product is -d/a = -c. (-1)(αβ) = -c => αβ = c = b - a + 1.

questions.text: A linear polynomial ax + b has exactly how many zeros?
questions.explanation: ax + b = 0 => x = -b/a. There is exactly one such value.

questions.text: The zeros of x² - 2x - 8 are:
If α and β are zeros of x² + x + 1, then α² + β² is:
The product of the zeros of 4x² + 3x + 12 is:
questions.explanation: Product = c/a = 12/4 = 3.

questions.text: If α and β are the zeros of the polynomial f(x) = x² - 5x + k such that α - β = 1, then k is:
Which of the following polynomials has -3 and 4 as its zeros?
questions.explanation: Sum = 1, Product = -12. Polynomial is k(x² - x - 12). If k=1/2, we get x²/2 - x/2 - 6.

questions.text: If the sum of zeros of x² - (k+6)x + 2(2k-1) is half their product, then k is:
Degree of a cubic polynomial is:
questions.explanation: A cubic polynomial is a polynomial of degree 3.

questions.text: If one zero of 3x² + 8x + k is the reciprocal of the other, then k is:
questions.explanation: Product of zeros α(1/α) = 1. So c/a = 1 => k/3 = 1 => k = 3.

questions.text: The division of a polynomial p(x) by a non-zero polynomial g(x) is possible only if deg(p(x)) is:
options: Greater than or equal to deg(g(x))
questions.explanation: To perform division, the degree of the dividend must be greater than or equal to the degree of the divisor.
